title: Psephellus ruprechtii
taxonomy: Psephellus ruprechtii is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places Psephellus ruprechtii in the order Asterales. Further classification includes the subfamily Carduoideae and the tribe Cardueae. Psephellus ruprechtii is a type of thistle, which is a characteristic shared with many other members of the Cardueae tribe. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Asteraceae family.

intro: Psephellus ruprechtii, also known as Ruprecht's dandelion or golden goat's beard, is a flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and lower blood pressure. The bioactive constituents of Psephellus ruprechtii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and menstrual cramps.
